title: logformatter: link by *task ID*, not build ID
body:
Reason: task IDs are unique and permanent; linking by
build ID and task name is non-unique, because Re-run.

Fixes: #14863

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
This is working and ready for review; @cevich PTAL (low priority).

How I verified:
```console
$ cirrus-pr-timing --download 14896       <--- private script, fetches all CI logs
$ egrep -ah '^  https.*artifac' cirrus-pr-timing.5309665809530880/*|wc -l
34   <--- yep, looks reasonable
[ run same thing without the wc -l ]
[ verify that the list looks reasonable, it does ]
[ click on every single link, confirm that it works ]
```
I even ran `machine` tests.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
**NOTE:*** I'm perfectly okay if this PR is merged w/ a failing `machine podman fedora-36 rootless host` - it has nothing directly  to do with the changes here.

LGTM
I concur with @cevich, however, I've restarted the tests to see if we get lucky.
@TomSweeneyRedHat we won't: podman-machine tests don't actually pass, that's why they're optional.
